info.title: Netstamp API
info.description: HTTP API contract for the Netstamp controller and probe runtime.

tags.name: Install
tags.description: Probe-agent install assets for Linux systemd hosts.

tags.name: Probe Runtime
tags.description: Probe-agent runtime handshake, polling, and result ingestion.

tags.name: Public Status Pages
tags.description: Public, shareable project status pages and their ordered elements.

tags.name: Alerts
tags.description: Project-scoped alert rules, alert incidents, and notifications.

tags.name: Probes
tags.description: Project-scoped probe registration and secret management.

tags.name: Assignments
tags.description: Selector previews and effective probe-check assignments for console users.

tags.name: Checks
tags.description: Ping, TCP, and traceroute check definitions.

tags.name: Labels
tags.description: Project-scoped key/value labels used by selectors.

tags.name: Project Invites
tags.description: Pending project invitations and invite responses.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Authenticated user profile, email, and password management.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: User registration, login, logout, and current-session access.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Instance-level administrator settings and bootstrap state.

get.description: Export Netstamp application data as a JSON backup. The export includes sensitive account and credential material such as password hashes and stored secrets.

post.description: Import a JSON backup produced by exportAdminData. Existing application data in the managed tables is replaced.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

get.description: Return instance-level settings visible to global administrators. Secret values are redacted.

patch.description: Update instance-level settings. Only global administrators can call this operation. SMTP passwords are write-only.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

get.description: Return users with instance-level administrator access. This does not include project membership or project permissions.

post.description: Grant instance-level administrator access to an existing user by email. This does not add the user to any project.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

404.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

delete.description: Revoke instance-level administrator access from a user. The current administrator cannot revoke themselves, and the system must keep at least one administrator.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

404.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

409.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the server.

get.description: Return all users visible to system administrators, including disabled accounts.

patch.description: Disable or re-enable a user account and grant or revoke instance-level administrator access.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

404.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

409.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the server.

post.description: Replace a user's password with a system administrator supplied value.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

404.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

get.description: Rotate and return the synchronizer CSRF token for the current authenticated session.

post.description: Create or resend an email verification link. The response does not reveal whether the email exists.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

patch.description: Consume a one-time email verification token and mark the account email as verified.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

post.description: Verify an email and password pair, then set an HTTP-only session cookie for the authenticated user. Invalid credentials always return the same unauthorized response. Unverified accounts return forbidden when email verification is required.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

post.description: Revoke the server-side session and clear the HTTP-only session cookie.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

get.description: Return the user identity associated with a valid session cookie.

post.description: Create a password reset request for an account email. The response does not reveal whether the email exists.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

patch.description: Consume a one-time password reset token and replace the account password.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

post.description: Create a user account with a normalized email address, display name, and password. When email verification is disabled, sets an HTTP-only session cookie for immediate API access. When email verification is required, sends a verification email and returns 202 without a session cookie.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

409.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the server.

host.description: SMTP server hostname. Empty means email delivery is disabled unless an environment fallback is still active.

username.description: SMTP username. Empty means no SMTP AUTH.

passwordSet.description: Whether a password is configured. The password value is never returned.

configured.description: Whether Netstamp has enough SMTP settings to attempt email delivery.

password.description: New SMTP password. Omit to keep the stored password unchanged.

clearPassword.description: Clear the stored SMTP password and override any environment fallback with no password.

examples.name: API packet loss
examples.description: Page the team when API ping loss is high.

csrfToken.description: One-time readable CSRF token to send in X-CSRF-Token for state-changing session-authenticated requests.

newEmail.description: New normalized email address to use for sign-in.

password.description: Current password used to confirm this sensitive account change.

currentPassword.description: Current password to verify before changing credentials.

newPassword.description: New password. It is stored only as an Argon2id hash.

examples.name: api-latency
examples.description: Latency and loss to controller API.

check.name: api-latency
check.description: Latency and loss to controller API.

token.description: One-time verification token from the email verification message.
One-time reset token from the password reset email.

newPassword.description: New password. It is stored only as an Argon2id hash.

examples.name: API packet loss
examples.description: Page the team when API ping loss is high.

examples.name: api-latency
examples.description: Latency and loss to controller API.

email.description: Email address for the account that should receive a new verification link.
Email address for the account that should receive a password reset link.
Existing user email to grant instance-level administrator access.
Email address used to sign in. It is normalized before lookup.

password.description: Plain-text password to verify. It is never returned by the API.

email.description: Normalized email address used to sign in.

emailVerified.description: Whether the sign-in email address has been verified.

disabledAt.description: When the user account was disabled. Omitted for active users.

isSystemAdmin.description: Whether the user has instance-level administrator access.

grantedAt.description: When the user received instance-level administrator access.

secret.description: Plaintext probe secret. It is returned only at creation and rotation time.

code.description: Stable machine-readable problem code. Clients should branch on this field instead of title or detail.
Stable machine-readable code for this field or sub-error.

message.description: Human-readable explanation for this field or sub-error.

location.description: Path-like location such as body.name, query.probeId, or path.ref.

value.description: Echoed invalid value, when the server chooses to return it.

examples.message: expected required property name to be present

email.description: Email address used to sign in.

password.description: Plain-text password. It is stored only as an Argon2id hash.

Selector.description: Selector AST used to match probes by project labels. Empty object matches all probes.

selector.description: Selector AST to validate and preview against active project probes. Empty or omitted selectors match all probes.

password.description: New password. It is stored only as an Argon2id hash.

email.description: Normalized email address used to sign in.

emailVerified.description: Whether the sign-in email address has been verified.

grantedAt.description: When the user received instance-level administrator access.

examples.errorMessage: destination was not reached before max hops

UpdateAlertRuleRequest.description: Full replacement patch for an alert rule in the current beta API.

examples.name: API packet loss
examples.description: Page the team when API ping loss is high.

UpdateCheckRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field must be provided.

UpdateCurrentUserRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field must be provided.

UpdateLabelRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field must be provided.

disabled.description: Disable or re-enable the account. Disabled accounts cannot sign in or access protected routes.

systemAdmin.description: Grant or revoke instance-level administrator access.

UpdateManagedUserRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field should be provided.

UpdateNotificationRequest.description: Full replacement patch for a notification in the current beta API.

UpdateProbeRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field must be provided.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Patch payload. At least one field must be provided.

UpdatePublicStatusElementRequest.description: Full replacement patch for a public status page element.

UpdatePublicStatusPageRequest.description: Full replacement patch for public status page settings.

email.description: Normalized email address used to sign in.

emailVerified.description: Whether the sign-in email address has been verified.

isSystemAdmin.description: Whether the user has instance-level administrator access.

ProbeAuth.name: Authorization
ProbeAuth.description: Probe runtime credential header. Send the full value as Authorization: Probe <secret>.
